cli.welcome: Welcome to VulnClaw! Enter a natural-language task to start. Type help for commands. Press Ctrl+C to exit.
cli.no_api_key: [!] No LLM API key detected. Run [bold]vulnclaw config set llm.api_key <your-key>[/] first.
cli.offline_mode: [dim]Starting in offline mode: local tools only, no AI reasoning.[/]
cli.press_again: [dim]Press Ctrl+C again within 3 seconds to exit REPL[/]
cli.no_target_for_report: [!] Set a target first with [bold]target <host>[/] or run [bold]report <host>[/].
cli.enter_auto_mode: [dim][*] Entering autonomous pentest mode. Press Ctrl+C to interrupt.[/]
cli.auto_mode_hint: [dim][*] Auto mode stays active. Type [bold]chat[/] to switch to single-turn mode.[/]
cli.auto_resume_hint: [dim]Press [bold]Enter[/] to resume auto pentest, or type a new command.[/]
cli.init.step_provider: 1. Choose a provider: [bold]vulnclaw config provider minimax[/]
cli.init.step_api_key: 2. Set API key: [bold]vulnclaw config set llm.api_key <your-key>[/]
help.title: VulnClaw Help
help.report: [cyan]report <host>[/]      Generate a report from the current/selected target
help.think_on_off: [cyan]think on/off[/]       Explicitly show or hide thinking
help.persistent: [cyan]persistent[/]         Start persistent testing for the current target
help.persistent_host: [cyan]persistent <host>[/]  Start persistent testing for a specific target
help.chat: [cyan]chat / manual[/]       Exit auto mode, switch to single-turn chat
help.auto_mode_desc: [dim]Enter a natural-language task that includes a target and VulnClaw will run autonomously.[/]
help.auto_mode_stays: [dim]Auto mode persists after completion. Type [bold]chat[/] to exit.[/]
help.persistent_mode_desc: [dim]Runs multi-round autonomous testing, generates reports, and continues across cycles.[/]
status.title: Current Status
auto_pentest.title: Autonomous Pentest Result
persistent.title: Persistent Pentest
tui.title: VulnClaw TUI Workbench
tui.desc: Terminal graphical entry for authorized security testing: Confirm target and boundaries before starting.
tui.mode_quick_desc: Only recon and basic risk identification. Suitable for first-time assessment.
tui.mode_standard_desc: Recon + vulnerability discovery. Default recommendation. No active high-risk verification.
tui.mode_deep_desc: More thorough vulnerability verification. Requires authorization confirmation.
tui.mode_continuous_desc: Multi-round continuous testing. Suitable for CTF or long-term monitoring.
tui.cli_note: Original CLI/REPL is still the default; TUI only activates with vulnclaw tui.
tui.press_again: [dim]Press Ctrl+C again within 3 seconds to exit TUI[/]
tui.diagnostic_footer: This is an inline TUI diagnostic summary. Run vulnclaw doctor for full details.
tui.enter_target: Enter authorized target URL / domain / IP
tui.select_provider: Select Provider (leave empty to keep current)
tui.api_key: API Key (leave empty to keep current)
tui.enter_allowed_actions: Allowed actions (comma-separated, leave empty for mode defaults)
tui.enter_blocked_actions: Blocked actions (comma-separated, leave empty for mode defaults)
tui.confirm_deep_mode: {mode} may perform deeper or multi-round verification. Confirm target and scope are authorized?
tui.name: Name
tui.enter_api_key: API Key (leave empty to keep current)
tui.task_returned: Task returned, press Enter to go back to TUI
tui.enter_task_mode: [dim][*] Entering security check mode. Press Ctrl+C to interrupt.[/]
tui.no_history_for_target: No history for this target yet.

tui.component.chat_input.placeholder: Enter / for commands, or type a message...
tui.component.scan_config.title: Security Scan Configuration
tui.screen.main.help_desc: Show all commands or detailed help for a specific command
tui.screen.main.help_detail:
List all available commands or show detailed usage for a specific command.

No arguments: list all available commands
/help <command>: show detailed help including usage, parameter descriptions and examples
/<command> /? or /<command> -h: shorthand, same as above

Examples:
  /help config    View help for the config command
  /sc /?          View help for the sc command
tui.screen.main.sc_detail:
Scan configuration management - configure and execute penetration testing tasks.

No arguments: open the graphical configuration panel
show: display current SC configuration in chat
run: execute the task with current configuration

Parameters:
  --target <target>       Set the test target (URL/IP)
  --mode <mode>           Set test mode: quick / standard / deep / continuous
  --only-host <host>      Restrict testing to this host only
  --only-port <port>      Restrict testing to this port only
  --only-path <path>      Restrict testing to this path only
  --blocked-host <host>   Exclude this host from testing
  --blocked-path <path>   Exclude this path from testing
  --allow-actions <act>   Allow specific actions (comma-separated)
  --block-actions <act>   Block specific actions (comma-separated)
tui.screen.main.config_desc: View/set configuration (LLM API, render mode, etc.)
tui.screen.main.config_detail:
Configuration management - view and modify VulnClaw settings.

No arguments: show all configuration (LLM, render, popup, etc.)
/config llm                Show LLM configuration details
/config llm set provider <name>   Switch LLM provider (e.g. deepseek, openai)
/config llm set api_key <key>     Set API Key
/config llm set base_url <url>    Set API Base URL
/config llm set model <name>      Set model name
/config render             Show current render mode
/config render markdown|plain    Toggle Markdown rendering
/config popup-mode         Show current popup mode
/config popup-mode embed|separate  Toggle popup mode (embedded/separate window)
tui.screen.main.clear_detail:
Clear all conversation history and context in the current session.

Note: This does not affect saved target state or configuration files.
It only removes all sent messages and LLM replies from the current session.
tui.screen.main.load_detail:
Load a previous session history from a file.

Argument: filename (optional file path)
Files are usually stored in ~/.vulnclaw/sessions/ directory.
After loading, you can view and continue the previous conversation.
tui.screen.main.save_detail:
Save the current session to a file.

After saving, you can use /load to restore it in a future session.
The file will be saved in the sessions directory with a timestamp.
tui.screen.main.exit_detail:
Exit VulnClaw TUI.

Equivalent to pressing Ctrl+C twice when not busy.
If a task is running, interrupt it first before exiting.
tui.screen.main.quit_detail:
Exit VulnClaw TUI.

Alias for /exit, behaves identically.
tui.screen.main.hint_bar: [dim]Type / for commands  |  Tab to complete  |  Ctrl+C to quit  |  /help for help[/]
tui.screen.main.welcome: [dim]Welcome to VulnClaw! Type /help to see available commands[/]
tui.screen.main.cancelled_notify: Operation cancelled, press Ctrl+C again to quit
tui.cli.tui.invalid_port_range: Port must be a number between 1-65535
tui.tool.bash.description: Execute a PowerShell command on Windows and capture its output (Note: system is Windows, use PowerShell syntax, uname/$(...) and other Linux/bash commands are not supported)
tui.agent.context.system_prompt:
You are VulnClaw, an AI-powered penetration testing assistant.
You can help users with security analysis, vulnerability detection, and penetration testing.
Please answer the user's questions in Chinese.
tui.service.llm.tool_fallback: [dim]Current model does not support tool calling (thinking mode may be enabled), falling back to text-only mode...[/]
tui.service.llm.syntax_hint: Do not use uname, $(...), or other Linux/bash-specific commands.
tui.service.llm.system_prompt: You are VulnClaw, an AI-powered penetration testing assistant. You can help users with security analysis, vulnerability detection, and penetration testing. You can use various tools to perform tasks. Note: The current system is {os}. Please use {shell} syntax when executing commands. {hint}Please answer in Chinese.

Open the graphical configuration panel for all settings: LLM provider, API key, model parameters, display mode, and session preferences.

No arguments needed.
